title: Intensely Roasted Japanese Fennel Ochazuke
description: A solo dinner Japanese dish featuring roasted fennel bulb, this sugar-free-friendly ochazuke is perfect for a weeknight meal, with a cheese-pull effect from the melted cheese.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the fennel bulb into 1-inch wedges and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the sesame oil over the fennel and sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Roast the fennel in the oven for 25 minutes, or until tender and caramelized.
In a small saucepan, heat the green tea over low heat.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked Japanese rice, roasted fennel, and shredded mozzarella cheese.
Pour the hot green tea over the rice mixture and stir until the cheese is melted and the mixture is well combined.
Stir in the ume plum vinegar and serve immediately.
